methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $500M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: 1X Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $10B

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: Fairmat
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $350M

private.name: GrayMatter Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $300M+

data.narrative:
Collaborative Robotics, founded by Brad Porter, is a Series B robotics and manufacturing company seeking valuation. The target currently carries a $500M+ post-money valuation on $150M+ raised. The comparable universe consists of 9 private peers in the same sector and stage, including 1X Technologies ($10.0B), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B), and Dexterity ($1.6B), alongside one public comparable.

Two triangulation methods apply. Stage-progression analysis uses the $500M last post-money valuation as the base and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, implying $950M. Peer-median analysis across the 5-company median yields $1.6B, with the comp set ranging from $300M (GrayMatter Robotics, Fairmat) to $10.0B (1X Technologies). The peer distribution is wide but anchored by three mid-range comps between $1.6B–$1.8B.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $808M–$1.9B, with a median of $1.3B. The $1.0B+ midpoint reflects the higher peer median relative to the stage-progression method. Key uncertainties include the extreme outlier positioning of 1X Technologies at $10.0B—which may reflect venture capital appetite for well-capitalized robotics rather than comparable execution—and the significant dispersion in the peer set ($300M floor to $10.0B ceiling). Without revenue or unit economics data, the range reflects stage and sector precedent rather than fundamental metrics.
